de.uka.ilkd.key.rule.join.procedures

Class JoinIfThenElseAntecedent

  • java.lang.Object
    • de.uka.ilkd.key.rule.join.JoinProcedure
      • de.uka.ilkd.key.rule.join.procedures.JoinIfThenElseAntecedent

  • public class JoinIfThenElseAntecedent
extends JoinProcedure
    Rule that joins two sequents based on the if-then-else construction: If two locations are assigned different values in the states, the value in the joined state is chosen based on the path condition. This rule retains total precision. In contrast to the JoinIfThenElse rule, the distinction is not realized in the update / symbolic state, but in the antecedent / path condition. This can be much more efficient.
    Author:
    Dominic Scheurer
    See Also:
    JoinIfThenElse, JoinRule

    • Method Detail

      • instance

        public static JoinIfThenElseAntecedent instance()

      • joinValuesInStates

        public Triple<ImmutableSet<Term>,Term,ImmutableSet<Name>> joinValuesInStates(Term v,
                                                                    SymbolicExecutionState state1,
                                                                    Term valueInState1,
                                                                    SymbolicExecutionState state2,
                                                                    Term valueInState2,
                                                                    Term distinguishingFormula,
                                                                    Services services)
        Description copied from class: JoinProcedure
        Joins two values valueInState1 and valueInState2 of corresponding SE states state1 and state2 to a new value of a join state.
        Specified by:
        joinValuesInStates in class JoinProcedure
        Parameters:
        v - The variable for which the values should be joined
        state1 - First SE state.
        valueInState1 - Value in state1.
        state2 - Second SE state.
        valueInState2 - Value in state2.
        distinguishingFormula - The user-specified distinguishing formula. May be null (for automatic generation).
        services - The services object.
        Returns:
        A joined value for valueInState1 and valueInState2, that is a triple consisting of new constraints, the actual value and new names introduced.

      • requiresDistinguishablePathConditions

        public boolean requiresDistinguishablePathConditions()
        Specified by:
        requiresDistinguishablePathConditions in class JoinProcedure
        Returns:
        true iff the join procedure requires distinguishable path conditions. This is usually the case for procedures working with concrete values of input states, and can be false for abstraction methods.

      • getIfThenElseConstraints

        private static ImmutableSet<Term> getIfThenElseConstraints(Term constrained,
                                          Term ifTerm,
                                          Term elseTerm,
                                          SymbolicExecutionState state1,
                                          SymbolicExecutionState state2,
                                          Term distinguishingFormula,
                                          Services services)
        Returns a list of if-then-else constraints for the given constrained term, states and if/else terms.
        Parameters:
        constrained - The constrained term.
        ifTerm - The value for the if case.
        elseTerm - The value for the else case.
        state1 - First SE state ("if").
        state2 - Second SE state ("else").
        distinguishingFormula - The user-specified distinguishing formula. May be null (for automatic generation).
        services - The services object.
        Returns:
        A list of if-then-else constraints for the given constrained term, states and if/else terms.

        public String toString()

        toString in class Object
